text: MMG describes Khoemacau as a long-life copper-silver mine in Botswana's Kalahari Copper Belt. The operation produces copper concentrate with silver by-product and is positioned as MMG's first operating asset in Africa.

sourceTitle: MMG to acquire Khoemacau Copper Mine
text: MMG's acquisition release described a 4,040 km2 tenement package with 6.4 Mt contained copper and 263 Moz contained silver in mineral resources. The release also highlighted expansion potential from the existing operation toward a larger copper-equivalent production profile.

Sandfire describes Motheo as an operating copper-silver mine in the central Kalahari Copper Belt. The operation includes the T3 and A4 open pits, the A1 development project and a central processing facility with demonstrated capacity of 5.6 Mtpa.

sourceTitle: Official Opening of the Motheo Copper Mine
text: Sandfire's opening announcement placed Motheo approximately 70 km north-east of Ghanzi and reported first copper concentrate production in May 2023. The release framed Motheo as a modern, long-life processing hub in Botswana's Kalahari Copper Belt.

Cobre describes Ngami as a 727 km2 copper-silver development opportunity in Botswana. The company highlights a 40 km strike of copper-silver mineralisation, chalcocite-dominant mineralisation and a conceptual scale of 103-166 Mt at 0.38-0.46% Cu in the exploration-target category.

sourceTitle: Cobre Insitu Copper Recovery Project Scoping Study
text: Cobre's 2024 scoping-study PDF evaluates an in-situ copper recovery concept at Ngami. The study links the project to the Kalahari Copper Belt and notes hydrogeological and metallurgical testwork supporting ISCR evaluation for copper-silver mineralisation.

sourceTitle: Kalahari Copper Belt Project - Galileo Resources
text: Galileo describes licences in Botswana's Kalahari Copper Belt located near active developments including Khoemacau, Motheo and Cobre's Ngami and Kitlanya projects. Its PL253/2018 licence is described as lying between Cobre's Kitlanya West and Ngami projects.

Bannerman describes Etango as a uranium project in Namibia's Erongo Region about 30 km southeast of Swakopmund. The page states a 207 Mlb contained U3O8 mineral resource at a 100 ppm U3O8 cut-off and notes the project is fully permitted.

Bannerman's resource and reserves page reports Etango mineral resources of 206.8 Mlb contained U3O8 at a 100 ppm cut-off and 224.9 Mlb at a 55 ppm cut-off. It also cites the 2022 Etango-8 DFS and a March 2024 scoping study expansion case.

Deep Yellow describes Tumas as a palaeochannel/calcrete uranium project in Namibia with a total resource of 137 Mlb U3O8 at 245 ppm and an ore reserve of 79.5 Mlb U3O8 at 298 ppm. The company notes 125 km of prospective palaeochannels and a potential 30-year mine life.

Deep Yellow's Namibia portfolio page positions Tumas within a broader Namibian uranium platform that also includes Omahola, Tubas and Nova. This provides regional context for Tumas as the company's lead Namibian development project.

Paladin describes Langer Heinrich Mine as a 75%-owned strategic uranium asset in central western Namibia, about 80 km east of Swakopmund and 85 km northeast of Walvis Bay. The company lists a conventional open-pit operation with a 17-year mine life.

sourceTitle: LHM Guidance Revision - Increase FY2026 Production Range
text: Paladin's April 2026 update reported strong Langer Heinrich ramp-up performance, including 3.6 Mlb U3O8 produced year-to-date in FY2026. The company revised FY2026 production guidance upward to 4.5-4.8 Mlb U3O8.

Forsys describes Norasa as comprising the Valencia and Namibplaas uranium deposits in Namibia's Erongo Region. The company states Valencia has Mining Licence ML-149 and Namibplaas is held under EPL-3638, with both deposits having NI 43-101 compliant resources.

sourceTitle: Forsys Reports Updated Mineral Resource Estimate for its Norasa Project in Namibia
text: Forsys' May 2024 resource update covers the Valencia and Namibplaas deposits that make up Norasa. The release reports updated mineral resource modelling based on recent confirmatory and geotechnical drilling together with historical resource data.
